stopConditions: Stop after collecting public official Queen Mary sources specific to AI use, academic integrity, data handling, or approved tools.
Do not access login-gated QMplus, SharePoint, Jira, or other restricted systems.
Do not treat draft, interview, or newsletter material as a binding university policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:qmul.ac.uk ("generative AI" OR "GenAI" OR "ChatGPT") (policy OR guidance OR assessment OR academic integrity)
methodsAttempted.notes: Found official Queen Mary staff, student, PGR, IT FAQ, policy index, and AI policy newsletter candidates.
Followed official guidance links to staff/student/PGR pages, the policy zone, AI FAQs, QMplus resources, and SharePoint approved-tool list.

methodsAttempted.query: Academic Integrity and Misconduct Policy 2025-26 PDF Queen Mary
methodsAttempted.notes: Fetched the current Academic Integrity & Misconduct Policy PDF listed on the official Policy Zone page.

methodsAttempted.query: Queen Mary official AI policy final review published approved
methodsAttempted.notes: Identified a Digital Education Studio newsletter/interview about an AI policy in final review; rejected as not the published policy.

artifacts.summary: Collected official public Queen Mary sources for AI education guidance, student use, PGR research use, IT/privacy/approved-tool guidance, and academic integrity policy. Login-gated QMplus and SharePoint resources were recorded as rejected candidates and not used as evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Queen Mary publishes an official AI education guidance page that links AI principles, staff guidance, student guidance, PGR guidance, academic integrity resources, and the Policy Zone rather than presenting the guidance page itself as a standalone binding AI policy.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Official AI education guidance hub links staff, student, PGR, academic integrity, and policy-zone resources

artifacts.sourceTitle: University AI principles, guidance and policies - Queen Mary Academy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Our innovative use of AI in education is supported by policy and guidance for educators and students. Guidance for staff and students: Staff Guide to Generative AI; Student Guide to Generative AI; Academic Integrity at Queen Mary; AI for student learning and research; AI guidance for PGRs.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Queen Mary frames AI in education as supported by policy and guidance, and links staff, student, PGR, academic integrity and Policy Zone resources.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: University AI principles, guidance and policies; Guidance for staff and students; University Policy
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Queen Mary staff guidance says open-access GenAI tools are not secure for institutional data, and directs staff to use institutional Microsoft Copilot for sensitive, confidential, or internal-use-only information while not sharing internal-use Queen Mary data with external AI tools.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Staff guidance directs sensitive/internal Queen Mary data to institutional Copilot, not external AI tools

citation.citationTitle: Staff Guide to Generative AI - Queen Mary Academy
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Staff Guide to Generative AI - Queen Mary Academy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It’s important to remember that data entered into open-access GenAI tools - including widely used platforms such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and others - is not secure. Use Microsoft Copilot when working with sensitive, confidential, or internal-use-only information. Do not share any Queen Mary data designated for internal use with external AI tools.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For staff, sensitive, confidential, or internal-use Queen Mary information should use institutional Copilot; internal-use data should not be shared with external AI tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Staff Guide to Generative AI; Privacy and data security
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI Guidance for PGRs - Doctoral College
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: With your supervisors, decide which system(s) you will use, why you will use them, and how you will use them. Keep detailed records of your inputs and outputs. Reference GenAI information and declare its use in your work.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The green shield icon confirms you’re using the Queen Mary secure version of Copilot. This means: Your data stays within the Queen Mary’s Microsoft 365 environment. It meets institutional security and data protection requirements. Inputs are not stored or used for external model training.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Integrity & Misconduct Policy 2025-26
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Academic Integrity & Misconduct Policy applies to all students at Queen Mary. Academic Integrity is essential in all areas of academic life. Actions that undermine integrity may be considered misconduct in any assessment or activity, including formative assessment or learning activities.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The academic integrity policy applies to all students and may cover misconduct in assessments or learning activities, including formative work.

artifacts.title: Queen Mary University of London AI Policy Source Review - 2026-05-14
artifacts.summary: Official Queen Mary public sources provide AI education guidance for staff, students, and PGRs; connect student AI use to academic integrity expectations; direct staff toward institutional Microsoft Copilot for sensitive/internal data; and require approval before using free AI tools with Queen Mary data. A newsletter page about an AI policy in final review was not used as binding policy evidence, and login-gated QMplus/SharePoint sources were recorded as rejected candidates.
limitations: This staged report is based only on public official Queen Mary University of London sources retrievable on 2026-05-14.
The Digital Education Studio article about a forthcoming AI policy was treated as newsletter/interview context and not as an approved policy source.
QMplus course content, SharePoint approved-tool lists, Jira/Ideas submissions, and other authenticated resources were not accessed or used as evidence.
This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
